Bead Watcher', 'Deploy Stage A process model — exactly one process, by design'. An operator running the documented uvicorn command must get the watcher for free; 'server up, watcher absent' must be impossible.\n\nScope:\n- Run the watcher loop as an asyncio task spawned in the FastAPI lifespan; crash-restart with backoff; last_tick_at stamping.\n- GET /health gains a watcher block: alive = task running AND last tick within 2x interval.\n- Detection is CLI-only: poll 'bf list --status closed' with a close-timestamp high-water mark (default 30s interval); NEVER read .beads/ files directly (SQLite db is the CLI's private store; issues.jsonl misses unflushed mutations).\n- On close: resolve the bead's intent via intents.bead_ref, read session_id from the intent row (beads carry no session metadata), write the results row, mark the intent resolved/failed, SSE push per Surface Routing Rules.\n\nAcceptance criteria:\n- /health shows watcher.alive true under normal run; killing the task shows false within 2x interval.\n- Integration test: close a test bead via bf -> results row + SSE event within one interval.\n- A repo grep-guard test asserts no code path opens .beads/ files directly.\nAssignee: claude-code-glm-5-adc1\nLabels: deferred, demo, failure-count:3, gap-review-20260722","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"0678395d-1d65-4e2d-b282-c0eb4feef6b7","uuid":"dab227d1-b6d9-48bc-ae40-4d8ae98a854b","timestamp":"2026-07-22T12:13:39.408Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"ID: adc-c847\nTitle: Bead watcher in-process: FastAPI lifespan task, CLI-only polling, /health watcher block, session via intents.bead_ref\nStatus: in_progress\nPriority: P1\nType: task\nDescription: Plan: docs/plan/plan.md — '10. Bead Watcher', 'Deploy Stage A process model — exactly one process, by design'. An operator running the documented uvicorn command must get the watcher for free; 'server up, watcher absent' must be impossible.\n\nScope:\n- Run the watcher loop as an asyncio task spawned in the FastAPI lifespan; crash-restart with backoff; last_tick_at stamping.\n- GET /health gains a watcher block: alive = task running AND last tick within 2x interval.\n- Detection is CLI-only: poll 'bf list --status closed' with a close-timestamp high-water mark (default 30s interval); NEVER read .beads/ files directly (SQLite db is the CLI's private store; issues.jsonl misses unflushed mutations).\n- On close: resolve the bead's intent via intents.bead_ref, read session_id from the intent row (beads carry no session metadata), write the results row, mark the intent resolved/failed, SSE push per Surface Routing Rules.\n\nAcceptance criteria:\n- /health shows watcher.alive true under normal run; killing the task shows false within 2x interval.\n- Integration test: close a test bead via bf -> results row + SSE event within one interval.\n- A repo grep-guard test asserts no code path opens .beads/ files directly.\nAssignee: claude-code-glm-5-adc1\nLabels: deferred, demo, failure-count:3, gap-review-20260722","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_c7e383035b5c49c19db70a3a","type":"tool_result","content":"plan.md\n---\n### 10. Bead Watcher\n\n**Runtime:** In-process daemon loop — an asyncio background task spawned by FastAPI lifespan startup, inside the same process as the server (see Deploy Stage A process model). No LLM\n\nWatches for bead close events from NEEDLE workers. Detection is CLI-only: the watcher polls `bf list --status closed` on a fixed interval (default 30s) and keeps a high-water mark (newest close timestamp already processed) to pick out new closures. On the same tick it checks each open tracked bead — every unresolved `intents.bead_ref` — via `bf show`, parsing comments past a per-bead high-water mark for `REFUSED:` entries (the circuit-breaker signal; see The Async Path). All `bf` invocations run from the aide-de-camp repo's beads workspace (see Beads-Workspace Scoping under the Escalate Strand). It never reads `.beads/` files directly — the SQLite db is the CLI's private store, and `issues.jsonl` is only a flush checkpoint that misses unflushed mutations; both are documented corruption/staleness footguns in this workspace.\n\nOn close:\n1. Resolves the closed bead to its intent via `intents.bead_ref` and reads `session_id` from the intent row — the bead itself carries no session metadata; the mapping lives entirely in the session store\n2. Looks up active surface in session store\n3. Writes result to `results` table and marks the intent `resolved` (or `failed`, per The Async Path's terminal-failure rule)\n4. Fires SSE push to canvas or Telegram push if no canvas active\n\n> ⚠ The Telegram push in step 4 is NON-FUNCTIONAL until ADR-1 lands — `src/telegram/fallback.py`'s delivery methods are stubs pointed at an unreachable bridge URL (see ADR-1). Today, a bead closing with no active canvas writes to `results` but notifies no one.\n\nThe same poll loop drives the async-path circuit breaker (see The Async Path): each tick it folds newly parsed refusal comments into `bead_watch` (refusal count, last reason, comment high-water mark), flags cards whose `sla_deadline` has passed, and fences stuck beads to `status=blocked`, flipping their intents to `stuck`. Breaker and SLA state live in `bead_watch` in the session store — never only in memory — so a watcher restart loses nothing.\n\n**Ambient monitoring tick.** The watcher daemon is also the runtime for ambient monitoring — there is no separate monitoring process. On its own timer (`tick_interval_seconds` in `config/monitoring.yaml`, default 300; the config hot-reloads per tick per the Hot-Reload Architecture), the daemon evaluates each rule in `config/monitoring.yaml` against its watched topic: `src/monitoring/ambient.py` runs the relevant fetch-matrix sources through the fetch strand, diffs the output against `topic_context_cache`, and when a rule fires writes a `results` row directly — `topic_id` set, `intent_id` NULL (system-originated, no utterance behind it), `urgency` and exception type taken from the rule via `config/monitoring.yaml` and `config/exceptions.yaml`, `summary` filled from the rule's deterministic template (no LLM anywhere on the tick). From there the result enters Surface Routing Rules like any other: SSE push to the active surface, exception-class handling when urgency is critical.\n\nPure I/O. Not a separately started process: FastAPI's lifespan startup spawns the watcher loop in-process, so the single Stage A run command brings up hot path, watcher, ambient monitoring, and circuit breaker together — the server cannot be up with the watcher silently absent. Liveness is verified, not assumed: the watcher stamps `last_tick_at` after every poll tick, and `GET /health` exposes a `watcher` block (`alive`, `last_tick_at`, tick count) where `alive` is true only while the task is running **and** `last_tick_at` is within 2× the poll interval. A crashed watcher task is logged and restarted with backoff by the lifespan supervisor; the `/health` gap makes the crash observable. Under Deploy Stage B the same in-process model ships inside the aide-de-camp Deployment (never a K8s Job/CronJob, per infrastructure conventions).\n\n---\n\n## Degraded-State UX\n\n\"Failed sources are surfaced as caveats\" is not a design. Every failure mode below maps to a **designed error card rendered from a fixed template** — these templates ship as built-in cards in the served frontend, the fourth built-in family (see Component Library → Built-in cards), filled client-side from SSE error events — so the canvas never shows a blank region, a spinner that never resolves, raw JSON, or a stack trace. These cards are what the Phase 5 known-issues register's degraded-state row points at.\n\n| Failure mode | Canvas presentation | Recovery action |\n|--------------|---------------------|-----------------|\n| One fetch source fails or times out | Normal card built from the surviving sources; a caveat strip (from `fetch_coverage`) names what's missing — e.g. \"ArgoCD unreachable — sync status omitted\" | Per-source retry on the caveat; the next dispatch on the topic refetches everything |\n| ALL fetch sources fail | \"No data\" error card: intent header + per-source failure list. Fixed template, no LLM call — synthesize is skipped, there is nothing to synthesize | Retry button re-runs fetch for the intent; utterance and intent are preserved |\n| ZAI proxy down / timeout / quota-exhausted at the **router** stage | Dispatch-level error card: \"Router unavailable — LLM proxy unreachable\", with the raw utterance shown so nothing is lost | Retry re-dispatches the persisted utterance. ⚠ No fallback LLM endpoint exists — a proxy outage kills the entire hot path (router **and** synthesize); a direct-API fallback is tracked as Open Question 8 |\n| ZAI proxy failure at the **synthesize** stage | Degraded \"raw data\" card: the structured fetch output renders under a \"summary unavailable\" banner — fetched data is never discarded | Retry-synthesize action reuses the fetched context (no refetch) |\n| SSE connection drops | Unobtrusive \"reconnecting…\" indicator; existing cards stay visible, marked stale | Client auto-reconnects (EventSource backoff + `Last-Event-ID`); on reconnect the workload-summary replay delivers anything surfaced while disconnected |\n| Hot-path card still pending at 30s (server hung, dispatch lost — no SSE event will ever arrive) | Aged-pending treatment applied by the **canvas client itself**: the card exists to age at all because the canvas creates a local per-utterance pending placeholder at submit time, before any server response (split into per-thread pending cards on the dispatch ack — see Escalate Strand, Pending/ack card render path); a client-side timer from the placeholder's creation, no server dependency, shows elapsed time plus \"taking longer than expected\" (see The Async Path — Visible aging) | Retry re-dispatches the card's utterance; the aged card is replaced by the retry's result |\n| Router returns malformed JSON / schema-invalid output | After one automatic corrective retry, a clarification-style card: \"Couldn't parse that into intents\", showing the utterance with an edit-and-resend action. Raw model output goes to logs, never the canvas | User edits or resends; malformed output is logged as fodder for router-prompt iteration |\n\nQuota exhaustion on the ZAI proxy presents identically to the proxy being down — same rows, same cards. The proxy is a single point of failure for the entire hot path; that is a deliberate Deploy Stage A trade-off (tracked as Open Question 8), and it stays visible here rather than hidden.\n\n---\n\n## Data Model\n\n### Session Store (SQLite)\n\n```sql\nsessions (\n  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  created_at  INTEGER,\n  last_active INTEGER,\n  primary_surface_id TEXT\n)\n\nsurfaces (\n  id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  session_id      TEXT,\n  type            TEXT,  -- 'canvas' | 'telegram' | 'audio'\n  state           TEXT,  -- 'active' | 'idle' | 'disconnected'\n  always_available INTEGER DEFAULT 0,  -- 1 for Telegram (⚠ aspirational: Telegram delivery non-functional; ADR-1 removes session-bound Telegram surfaces)\n  last_seen       INTEGER\n)\n\nutterances (\n  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  session_id  TEXT,\n  raw_text    TEXT,\n  created_at  INTEGER\n)\n\nintents (\n  id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  utterance_id TEXT,\n  session_id   TEXT,\n  topic_id     TEXT,  -- primary topic, authoritative and always set; see intent_topics\n  project_slug TEXT,\n  intent_type  TEXT,\n  lookup_kind  TEXT,  -- lookup intents only: 'logs' | 'config' | 'docs' (router-emitted; see Intent Router). NULL otherwise\n  status       TEXT,  -- 'pending' | 'dispatched' | 'resolved' | 'stuck' | 'failed' | 'cancelled'\n  bead_ref     TEXT,  -- set for task-profile intents\n  created_at   INTEGER,\n  resolved_at  INTEGER","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"0678395d-1d65-4e2d-b282-c0eb4feef6b7","uuid":"a32d704a-adae-45f7-afd8-db7d3f516ab0","timestamp":"2026-07-22T12:13:49.195Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"plan.md\n---\n### 10. Bead Watcher\n\n**Runtime:** In-process daemon loop — an asyncio background task spawned by FastAPI lifespan startup, inside the same process as the server (see Deploy Stage A process model). No LLM\n\nWatches for bead close events from NEEDLE workers. Detection is CLI-only: the watcher polls `bf list --status closed` on a fixed interval (default 30s) and keeps a high-water mark (newest close timestamp already processed) to pick out new closures. On the same tick it checks each open tracked bead — every unresolved `intents.bead_ref` — via `bf show`, parsing comments past a per-bead high-water mark for `REFUSED:` entries (the circuit-breaker signal; see The Async Path). When\n278\t        no chat id is configured this is a graceful no-op: a WARNING is logged\n279\t        and False is returned, matching send_exception() /\n280\t        send_workload_summary() and the pre-config behavior. 'created', 'updated', 'closed', 'commented'\n    timestamp: int\n    data: dict\n\n\nclass BeadWatcher:\n    \"\"\"\n    Watches for bead events and routes results to surfaces.\n\n    Integration with the bf CLI (bead-forge):\n    - Reads the bf workspace checkpoint JSONL (`.beads/issues.jsonl`) for\n      terminal beads\n    - Extracts session_id / origin_surface_id / urgency from the bead's flat\n      `labels` array -- escalate/handler.py encodes these as `key=value` labels\n    - Pushes results to active surfaces via SSE\n    - Falls back to Telegram if no surface available\n\n    The checkpoint is rewritten in full on every `bf sync --flush-only` (it is\n    not append-only), so each tick re-reads the whole file and relies on\n    `_processed_beads` to dedup already-delivered beads.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS = 5\n    # bf (bead-forge) workspace checkpoint. The live store is beads.db; this is\n    # the flushed checkpoint bf writes on `bf sync --flush-only`. Absolute path\n    # because the server may be launched from any CWD (matches DB_PATH in\n    # src/main.py). The old value \".beads/beads.jsonl\" never existed -- this file\n    # is the real one and has always been named issues.jsonl.\n    BEADS_JSONL = \"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/.beads/issues.jsonl\"\n    # Terminal statuses meaning \"work is done, deliver the result\". bf uses\n    # \"closed\" for normally-completed beads and \"resolved\" for beads closed via\n    # the escalate auto-approve path. Both warrant delivery (see adc-5wtm); the\n    # session_id-label guard below scopes delivery to escalate-tracked beads\n    # regardless of status, so including \"resolved\" is safe.\n    TERMINAL_STATUSES = (\"closed\", \"resolved\")\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        store: SessionStore,\n        router: SurfaceRouter,\n        beads_jsonl: str | None = None,\n        telegram_fallback: TelegramFallback | None = None,\n    ):\n        self.store = store\n        self.router = router\n        # Allow tests / alternate workspaces to point at a scratch checkpoint.\n        self._beads_jsonl = beads_jsonl or self.BEADS_JSONL\n        # Telegram delivery surface. Defaults to None and is resolved lazily to\n        # the shared singleton (get_telegram_fallback) on first send, so the\n        # bead watcher uses the SAME configured chat id, bridge URL,\n        # reachability, and failure-tracking state as send_exception() /\n        # send_workload_summary() in src/telegram/fallback.py. Injectable so\n        # tests can drive the delivery path with a fake. (adc-372c)\n        self._telegram_fallback = telegram_fallback\n        self._running = False\n        self._task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = None\n        # Bead IDs we have already delivered. In-memory only, so a process\n        # restart re-delivers currently-terminal beads (pre-existing behavior).\n        self._processed_beads: set[str] = set()\n\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Start the bead watcher daemon.\"\"\"\n        self._running = True\n        self._task = asyncio.create_task(self._watch_loop())\n        logger.info(\"Bead watcher started\")\n\n    async def stop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Stop the bead watcher daemon.\"\"\"\n        self._running = False\n        if self._task:\n            self._task.cancel()\n            try:\n                await self._task\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                pass\n        logger.info(\"Bead watcher stopped\")\n\n    async def _watch_loop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Main watch loop - checks for new bead events.\"\"\"\n        while self._running:\n            try:\n                await self._check_for_events()\n                await asyncio.sleep(self.CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS)\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                break\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error in bead watch loop: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n                await asyncio.sleep(self.CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS)\n\n    async def _check_for_events(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Check for terminal bead events and process them.\"\"\"\n        beads_path = Path(self._beads_jsonl)\n        if not beads_path.exists():\n            return\n\n        events = await self._read_terminal_events(beads_path)\n\n        for event in events:\n            await self._process_bead_event(event)\n\n    async def _read_terminal_events(self, beads_path: Path) -> list[BeadEvent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Read terminal (closed/resolved) bead events from the checkpoint.\n\n        The bf checkpoint is rewritten in full on each flush (not appended to),\n        so byte-offset tracking would silently miss beads whose status changed\n        on an earlier line. We therefore re-read the whole file each tick;\n        `_processed_beads` guards against re-delivering beads already routed.\n        \"\"\"\n        events: list[BeadEvent] = []\n\n        try:\n            with open(beads_path, \"r\") as f:\n                lines = f.readlines()\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Error reading beads file: {e}\")\n            return events\n\n        for line in lines:\n            if not line.strip():\n                continue\n\n            try:\n                bead = json.loads(line)\n            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n                logger.warning(f\"Failed to parse bead JSON: {e}\")\n                continue\n\n            bead_id = bead.get(\"id\")\n            status = bead.get(\"status\")\n\n            if not bead_id or bead_id in self._processed_beads:\n                continue\n\n            # Process terminal beads (closed or resolved)\n            if status in self.TERMINAL_STATUSES:\n                events.append(BeadEvent(\n                    bead_id=bead_id,\n                    event_type=status,  # 'closed' or 'resolved'\n                    timestamp=int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n                    data=bead,\n                ))\n                self._processed_beads.add(bead_id)\n\n        return events\n\n    def _extract_metadata(self, bead: dict) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Reconstruct routing metadata from a bead's flat `labels` array.\n\n        escalate/handler.py's `_create_bead` / `_create_bead_with_type` encode\n        session_id, origin_surface_id, urgency, etc. as `key=value` label\n        strings (e.g. `session_id=session-1`). bf issues have NO nested\n        `metadata` object -- only a flat `labels` list -- so we parse those\n        `key=value` entries back into a dict, matching the encoding the escalate\n        handler writes. Labels without `=` (e.g. `deferred`, `split-child`) and\n        `:`-style labels (e.g. `failure-count:1`) are ignored.\n        \"\"\"\n        metadata: dict = {}\n        labels = bead.get(\"labels\", [])\n        if not isinstance(labels, list):\n            return metadata\n        for label in labels:\n            if not isinstance(label, str) or \"=\" not in label:\n                continue\n            key, _, value = label.partition(\"=\")\n            key = key.strip()\n            if key:\n                metadata[key] = value.strip()\n        return metadata\n\n    async def _process_bead_event(self, event: BeadEvent) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Process a bead event and route to surfaces.\"\"\"\n        bead_data = event.data\n\n        # Extract routing metadata from the bead's labels (escalate encoding).\n        metadata = self._extract_metadata(bead_data)\n        session_id = metadata.get(\"session_id\")\n        surface_id = metadata.get(\"origin_surface_id\")\n\n        if not session_id:\n            # Not an escalate-tracked bead (no session_id label) -- nothing to\n            # route. This is expected for the vast majority of bf beads, so this\n            # is a debug log, not a warning.\n            logger.debug(f\"Bead {event.bead_id} has no session_id label; skipping\")\n            return\n\n        # Determine target surface\n        decision = await self.router.route_result(\n            session_id=session_id,\n            origin_surface_id=surface_id,\n            urgency=metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n        )\n\n        # Extract result from bead\n        result = await self._extract_result_from_bead(bead_data, session_id)\n\n        if not result:\n            logger.warning(f\"Could not extract result from bead {event.bead_id}\")\n            return\n\n        # Route to target surfaces\n        if decision.target_surfaces:\n            for surface in decision.target_surfaces:\n                if surface.type == \"telegram\":\n                    await self._send_to_telegram(result, session_id)\n                else:\n                    # SSE broadcast\n                    await broadcast_result(\n                        result=result,\n                        session_id=session_id,\n                        target_surface_id=surface.id,\n                    )\n        elif decision.fallback_used:\n            # Fallback to Telegram\n            await self._send_to_telegram(result, session_id)\n        else:\n            # No surface available - result stays in queue\n            logger.info(f\"No surface available for bead {event.bead_id}, result queued\")\n\n    async def _extract_result_from_bead(self, bead: dict, session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Extract result data from a terminal bead (real bf schema).\"\"\"\n        metadata = self._extract_metadata(bead)\n\n        # bf stores the bead body in `description` (NOT `body`); fall back to\n        # notes then title so the summary is never empty.\n        body = bead.get(\"description\") or bead.get(\"notes\") or bead.get(\"title\") or \"\"\n\n        return {\n            \"id\": bead.get(\"id\"),\n            \"type\": \"bead_result\",\n            \"summary\": body[:200] if len(body) > 200 else body,  # Truncate for summary\n            \"data\": {\n                \"bead_id\": bead.get(\"id\"),\n                \"title\": bead.get(\"title\"),\n                \"issue_type\": bead.get(\"issue_type\"),\n                \"description\": body,\n                \"status\": bead.get(\"status\"),\n            },\n            \"urgency\": metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n            \"surfaced_at\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n        }\n\n    async def _send_to_telegram(self, result: dict, session_id: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Send a result to Telegram via the shared TelegramFallback singleton.\n\n        Uses the single configured chat id (ADC_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID) -- there is\n        intentionally NO multi-user session→chat mapping, since aide-de-camp is\n        a single-user personal app (plan.md Tech Stack: \"single-user app\"). When\n        no chat id is configured this is a graceful no-op: a WARNING is logged\n        and False is returned, matching send_exception() /\n        send_workload_summary() and the pre-config behavior. When configured,\n        the result is formatted via ``_format_telegram_message`` and delivered\n        through ``send_message``, returning the bridge's real success/failure.\n        \"\"\"\n        fallback = self._telegram_fallback or get_telegram_fallback()\n\n        if fallback.chat_id is None:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"Cannot send result to Telegram for session {session_id}: \"\n                f\"no Telegram chat id configured (set ADC_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID). \"\n                f\"Result push skipped.\"\n            )\n            return False\n\n        message = self._format_telegram_message(result)\n        return await fallback.send_message(fallback.chat_id, message)\n\n    def _format_telegram_message(self, result: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Format result as Telegram message.\"\"\"\n        summary = result.get(\"summary\", \"Result available\")\n        urgency = result.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\")\n\n        emoji_map = {\n            \"critical\": \"🚨\",\n            \"high\": \"⚠️\",\n            \"normal\": \"📌\",\n            \"low\": \"💬\",\n        }\n\n        emoji = emoji_map.get(urgency, \"📌\")\n\n        # Build message\n        lines = [\n            f\"{emoji} {summary}\",\n        ]\n\n        # Add detail if available\n        data = result.get(\"data\", {})\n        if \"bead_id\" in data:\n            lines.append(f\"Bead: {data['bead_id']}\")\n\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\nasync def create_bead_watcher(store: SessionStore, router: SurfaceRouter) -> BeadWatcher:\n    \"\"\"Create and start a bead watcher daemon.\"\"\"\n    watcher = BeadWatcher(store, router)\n    await watcher.start()\n    return watcher\n","numLines":329,"startLine":1,"totalLines":329}}}
